dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the situation has escalated. They got their hands on inferior substitutes while you were out and tried to work with them anyway. Nearly destroyed everything.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} pulled them out before the worst happened. They were furious: not grateful, furious. Said they were close to a breakthrough on {quest_focus_the}. {quest_ally} found them trying to trade their last possessions for more materials.
dialogueChunks.outro: If you found the original {quest_item}, bring it now. They're past the point of reason and the next shortcut might be the one that kills them.

dialogueChunks.intro: They tried the substitutes. The result was visible from three settlements away. The workshop is still standing, but barely.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} found them in the wreckage, already mixing another batch with bleeding hands. They weren't even shaken: they were taking notes. {quest_ally} tried to reason with them and they said: 'I'm closer than anyone has ever been to {quest_focus_the}. You want me to stop now?' The {enemy_type} camp where {quest_item} was taken is still reachable, but the path is dangerous.
dialogueChunks.outro: Get {quest_item} before they try again with something worse. The next attempt might not leave anything standing.

dialogueChunks.intro: Their family came to see them yesterday. First time in years. They brought a grandchild they've never met.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: They barely looked up. {target_npc} says the family stood there for ten minutes before the tears started. {quest_ally} tried to intervene but they just said: 'After I find the last piece of {quest_focus_the}. Then I'll have time.'
dialogueChunks.outro: The final piece was last traced to {location_hint}. Find it. Not for them: for the family that's watching them disappear into {quest_focus_the} one day at a time.
